name: Shrimp and Sausage Jambalaya
description: The ultimate Southern one-pot meal — smoky sausage, shrimp, chicken, and rice all simmered together with bold Cajun spice.
recipeIngredient: 1 pound boneless skinless chicken thighs, diced
1 pound large shrimp, peeled and deveined
recipeInstructions: Brown the meats: Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown 8 ounces andouille sausage, sliced until slightly crispy, 3–4 min. Remove and set aside. Season 1 pound boneless skinless chicken thighs, diced with half the 2 teaspoons Cajun seasoning and brown in the same pot, 4–5 min. Remove and set aside.

Sauté the trinity: Add 1 yellow onion, diced, 1 green bell pepper, diced, and 2 celery stalks, diced to the pot. Cook over medium heat until softened, about 5 min. Add 4 garlic cloves, minced and cook 1 min.

Build the jambalaya: Stir in 1 can diced tomatoes (14.5 oz), 2.5 cups chicken broth, 1.5 cups long-grain white rice, rinsed, remaining 2 teaspoons Cajun seasoning, and 1 teaspoon smoked paprika. Return sausage and chicken to the pot. Bring to a boil.
Simmer covered: Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and cook 20–22 min until rice is cooked and liquid is absorbed.
Add the shrimp: Stir in 1 pound large shrimp, peeled and deveined, cover, and cook 3–5 min until shrimp are pink and curled. Remove from heat.
Serve: Taste and adjust seasoning. Garnish with 3 green onions for garnish and serve directly from the pot.
